title: LoRA · Low-Rank Adapter Playground
tagline: Slide the rank, watch trainable parameters shrink 500×
summary: LoRA injects two small matrices A and B next to a frozen base weight. B·A is low-rank by construction — so fine-tuning a 70B model becomes fine-tuning two 32k-param matrices per layer. Slide the rank, see the trade-off between capacity and cost in real numbers.
highlights: Slide rank r from 1 to 64 — see trainable params drop from thousands to millions to billions.
Toggle model size (1B, 7B, 70B) and see storage savings.
Compare to full fine-tuning, Adam optimizer state, and LoRA savings.
QLoRA preview — what's different about 4-bit quantized base.
